rawResponse:
ANALYSIS:
1. The failing test at line 53 checks if the pay button has `type="submit"` attribute.
2. The HTML shows `<button id="pay-button">Pay</button>` - the button element exists but has NO `type` attribute at all (received "" in the error).
3. The button in the HTML is missing the `type="submit"` attribute that the test expects.
4. The locator `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Pay' })` correctly finds the button element - the issue is the test expectation doesn't match the HTML.

EXPLANATION:
The button element in the HTML source lacks the `type="submit"` attribute that the test is asserting. The test expects a submit button but the HTML only defines a plain button without any type attribute. I fixed this by adding `type="submit"` to the button element in the HTML so it properly represents a submit button and matches the test expectation.

ROOT_CAUSE_LOCATION:
APP_SOURCE

ORIGINAL BLOCK:
```html
        <!-- The test will expect id="pay-button" -->
        <button id="pay-button">Pay</button>
```

FIXED BLOCK:
```html
        <!-- The test will expect id="pay-button" -->
        <button id="pay-button" type="submit">Pay</button>
```
selectorBefore:
<!-- The test will expect id="pay-button" -->
        <button id="pay-button">Pay</button>
selectorAfter:
<!-- The test will expect id="pay-button" -->
        <button id="pay-button" type="submit">Pay</button>
explanation: The button element in the HTML source lacks the `type="submit"` attribute that the test is asserting. The test expects a submit button but the HTML only defines a plain button without any type attribute. I fixed this by adding `type="submit"` to the button element in the HTML so it properly represents a submit button and matches the test expectation.
